授業目的: この科目の目的は、ヨーロッパ諸国、特に欧州連合（EU）加盟国の国際関係について、学生が主体的に学ぶことにあります。現代ヨーロッパの歴史的背景を踏まえ、政治的、社会経済的、安全保障上の課題を探求することを目指します。
到達目標: 1. 学生は、ヨーロッパの独自の特徴と国際政治における役割について、特に、経済と政治システム、および文化的認識に焦点をあてて主体的に学びます。2. ヨーロッパでの危機がどのように発生し、それがどのように対処され、解決されたのか、その結果およびEUへの影響について考察します。3. ヨーロッパの継続的に変化する政治的および経済的課題を学ぶことによって、分析的、批判的、問題解決能力を向上させることが期待されます。
関連科目: Topics in International Politics A, Topics in International Politics B
授業の概要・背景: This course is centered on the international relations of European countries, especially the member states of the European Union (EU), adopting a historical reading of contemporary Europe and its political, socioeconomic, and security challenges. Lectures are structured around thematic topics rather than individual countries, encompassing Eastern and Western Europe. In this course, we will discuss issues affecting the European continent, including national populism, mass migration, economic stagnation, the war in Ukraine, EU separatism (Brexit), and Europe's relations with its neighbors and beyond.

学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic: Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy andcurriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school. By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.European Studies:1. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.2. Problem-solving skills: the ability to proactively identify problems and solve them based on appropriate methods.3. Intercultural understanding and multicultural coexistence: the ability to be sensitive to different cultures and to coexist with diverse cultures.
